# Month-End Close Checklist

## Overview

The monthly close process should be completed by the 10th business day of the following month. Maria Santos (part-time bookkeeper, 20 hours/week) handles steps 1-7. Rachel Green (CPA) reviews the completed close package and provides feedback by the 15th.

## Checklist

### 1. Bank Reconciliation
- [ ] Download bank statement from OnPoint Credit Union (available by 2nd business day)
- [ ] Reconcile checking account (1010) in QuickBooks — match every transaction
- [ ] Reconcile savings account (1020)
- [ ] Investigate and resolve any unreconciled items within 5 business days
- [ ] Common issues: POS deposits that span month-end, outstanding checks to food vendors
- **Target variance**: $0.00 (must reconcile to the penny)

### 2. Credit Card Statement Review
- [ ] Download Chase Ink statement (closes on 28th of each month)
- [ ] Verify all charges have receipts (Maria photographs receipts daily using Dext app)
- [ ] Categorize any uncategorized transactions in QuickBooks
- [ ] Flag any personal charges for reclassification to Owner's Draw (3100)
- [ ] Reconcile credit card liability account (2100)

### 3. Accounts Receivable Review
- [ ] Run AR aging report in QuickBooks
- [ ] Send reminder emails for invoices 15+ days past due
- [ ] Escalate invoices 45+ days past due to Rachel Green for collection letter
- [ ] Write off any invoices deemed uncollectible (requires Maria Gutierrez approval for amounts over $500)
- [ ] Record bad debt expense to account 6130

### 4. Accounts Payable
- [ ] Enter all vendor invoices received during the month
- [ ] Verify all invoices match purchase orders and delivery receipts
- [ ] Schedule payments per vendor terms (most vendors are Net 15 or Net 30)
- [ ] Key vendors: Sysco Foods (weekly delivery, Net 15), Stumptown Coffee Roasters (bi-weekly, Net 30), US Foods (weekly, Net 15)

### 5. Payroll Accrual
- [ ] Verify Gusto payroll reports match QuickBooks payroll entries
- [ ] Accrue wages for any days worked but not yet paid at month-end (Gusto runs bi-weekly, so there are typically 2-5 accrual days)
- [ ] Verify payroll tax liabilities are current
- [ ] Record employer portion of benefits (health insurance stipend: $200/month per full-time employee = $1,600/month)

### 6. Inventory Count
- [ ] Physical inventory count of food, beverage, and supplies (conducted last business day of month by kitchen manager, Roberto Flores)
- [ ] Compare to QuickBooks inventory balances
- [ ] Adjust for shrinkage, spoilage, and waste (target: under 3% of COGS)
- [ ] Record inventory adjustment entries
- **2025 average monthly shrinkage**: $380 (2.6% of COGS — within target)

### 7. Depreciation and Amortization
- [ ] Record monthly depreciation entries:
  - Kitchen equipment: $420/month (MACRS 5-year)
  - Furniture & fixtures: $180/month (MACRS 7-year)
  - Leasehold improvements: $250/month (straight-line over 10-year lease term)
- [ ] Total monthly depreciation: $850 to account 6100
- [ ] Note: New espresso machine ($18,000) — Section 179, full deduction in month of purchase (April 2026)

### 8. Revenue Recognition (Accrual Basis)
- [ ] Record any catering deposits received for future events as Deferred Revenue (liability account 2500)
- [ ] Recognize revenue for events that occurred during the month, moving from Deferred Revenue to appropriate revenue account (4030)
- [ ] Ensure POS daily sales match bank deposits (allow 1-2 day lag for credit card settlement)

### 9. Financial Statement Review
- [ ] Generate Profit & Loss statement (month and YTD)
- [ ] Generate Balance Sheet
- [ ] Compare to budget and prior year
- [ ] Prepare month-end summary memo for Maria Gutierrez (1 page: revenue, COGS %, notable expenses, cash position)
- [ ] Email close package to Rachel Green by 10th business day
